codeReview.summary: The feature correctly fixes the targeted get_record reload seam for plain RecordId-backed models, but it broadens repository-wide id normalization into nested payloads and changes plain-string RecordId compatibility in a way that can silently coerce non-id nested `id` fields into the model storage table. That exceeds the feature contract and risks corrupting decode semantics outside the reviewed ItRecordUser roundtrip path.

issues.description: `normalize_record_id_strings::<T>` recursively rewrites every nested object key named `id` using `T::storage_table()`. This is not limited to the root RecordId-backed model id that the feature is supposed to preserve. Any nested payload/object field with an `id` string under the decoded row can now be coerced into a `RecordId` for the root model table, which changes shared decode semantics beyond the targeted `Repo::get_record` seam and can mis-decode unrelated nested JSON shapes.

`deserialize_record_id_or_compat_string` now routes plain strings through `parse_record_id_or_plain_string(..., Some('_'))`, which turns arbitrary non-record-id strings into synthetic `_:` RecordIds instead of preserving the previous behavior of failing invalid strings. The feature brief says the remaining blocker is the get_record/decode reload seam for bound RecordIds; widening generic compat deserialization to accept any plain string changes broader RecordId parsing behavior without dedicated proof in this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker intentionally skipped the skill step to add/confirm a failing test from a clean baseline because the dirty tree already contained in-scope changes. The current skill mentions authoritative dirty baselines, but it still frames tests-first as the default path and does not clearly spell out how to review and constrain already-present implementation diffs for evidence-heavy follow-up features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 7906348d-e74d-4e89-90a9-901d50e91714: skillFeedback.deviations notes step 6-7 was bypassed because the dirty working tree was treated as authoritative.

sharedStateObservations.observation: GitNexus symbol lookup was partially unreliable for helper-level symbols (`decode_stored_row_value`, `deserialize_record_id_compat`), which forced fallback to targeted diff review even though GitNexus was installed. Shared guidance notes missing `detect_changes`, but not the helper-symbol lookup mismatch, so future reviewers may lose time retrying unsupported names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff verification for commit f6cbcd5c9b8d7a89d3e6f6739b0a65337f666d98 records `gitnexus impact decode_stored_row_value` and `gitnexus impact deserialize_record_id_compat` as unresolved despite the installed CLI being available.

summary: Fail: the commit fixes the specific ItRecordUser roundtrip but introduces broader recursive id coercion and generic plain-string RecordId acceptance outside the assigned seam.
